task_completion.description: Load the CT Cranium dataset into InVesalius.
task_completion.visual_evidence: InVesalius application window showing the loaded CT Cranium dataset, visible as skull cross-sections in the axial, coronal, and sagittal 2D viewers.

task_completion.description: Create a segmentation mask for 'Compact Bone (Adult)'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Masks' panel in InVesalius shows a newly created mask, and the thresholding preset is set to 'Compact Bone (Adult)'.

task_completion.description: Create a segmentation mask for 'Spongial Bone (Adult)'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Masks' panel in InVesalius shows another mask created, with the thresholding preset set to 'Spongial Bone (Adult)'.

task_completion.description: Generate 3D surfaces for both masks to determine their volumes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Surfaces' panel lists generated 3D surfaces for both masks, and the 3D viewer displays the rendered bone structures. Volume measurements (in mL or mm³) should be visible in the surface properties or a measurement dialog.

task_completion.description: Create a text report containing the volumes and their ratio.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the file `/home/ga/Documents/bone_report.txt` with the exact format: 'Compact Bone Volume: [Value] mL', 'Spongial Bone Volume: [Value] mL', and 'Ratio: [Value]'.

task_completion.description: Save the InVesalius project to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Project As' dialog showing the path `/home/ga/Documents/bone_analysis.inv3`, or the InVesalius window title bar updated to reflect the saved filename, or a file manager/terminal showing the file exists.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the InVesalius GUI to perform the segmentation and surface generation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through InVesalius menus, adjusting thresholds, and generating surfaces, rather than just running a pre-written script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The volume values reported are genuine and physically plausible.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values entered in the text report must be greater than 10.0 mL (as per anatomical expectations for a skull) and should match the values displayed in the InVesalius surface properties.

integrity.description: The text report was created by the agent using a text editor or standard command line tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor (like nano, gedit) or echo/cat commands being used to format and write the specific values into `bone_report.txt`.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct output paths are /home/ga/Documents/bone_analysis.inv3 for the project and /home/ga/Documents/bone_report.txt for the report. The minimum plausible volume for both compact and spongial bone in this dataset is 10.0 mL; values below this indicate incorrect segmentation or surface generation. The dataset is the official InVesalius v3.0 CT Cranium sample.
